title: Morris Inorder Traversal

hints: Morris traversal uses the concept of threaded binary trees.
The key idea is to temporarily modify the tree structure to create links (threads) that help traverse without stack.
For each node, if it has a left child, find the rightmost node in its left subtree (inorder predecessor).
If the predecessor's right pointer is null, make it point to current node (create thread) and move left.
If the predecessor's right pointer already points to current node (thread exists), remove the thread, visit current node, and move right.
If a node has no left child, visit it and move right.
This algorithm restores the tree structure after traversal by removing all created threads.
Time complexity is O(n) and space complexity is O(1) - no extra space for stack or recursion.
